payload.text: Let me start by exploring this repository to understand its structure and find a non-obvious convention or pattern.

input.prompt:
Explore this repository thoroughly. I need to find ONE specific, non-obvious convention, constraint, or pattern that would benefit future developers. Look for:
1. Anything that contradicts common Next.js defaults
2. Unusual file naming or directory structure conventions
3. Specific patterns in how components, routes, or API are organized
4. Any constraint on how code must be written

Start by reading the README, then explore the directory structure, look at actual code files (especially config files, next.config, package.json, tsconfig.json, and source files). Pay special attention to patterns that would NOT be obvious from just reading a README.

Report back with:
- The specific file(s) where you found this pattern/convention
- What the convention actually is
- Why it contradicts or differs from common Next.js defaults
- How it would affect future development if someone didn't know it

payload.content:
1	# LearnHub LMS
2	
3	A full-featured Learning Management System built with Next.js, Payload CMS, and PostgreSQL.
4	
5	## Vision
6	
7	LearnHub is a multi-tenant LMS platform where **organizations** can create and manage courses, **instructors** can build structured curricula with lessons, quizzes, and assignments, and **students** can enroll, track progress, earn certificates, and interact through discussions.
8	
9	## Architecture
10	
11	- **Frontend**: Next.js App Router with React Server Components
12	- **Backend/CMS**: Payload CMS (headless, admin panel at `/admin`)
13	- **Database**: PostgreSQL via `@payloadcms/db-postgres`
14	- **Auth**: JWT-based with role guard middleware (`student`, `instructor`, `admin`)
15	- **Rich Text**: Lexical editor for lesson content
16	- **Media**: File uploads via Payload Media collection (sharp for image processing)
17	
18	## Domain Model
19	
20	```
21	Organization (tenant)
22	├── Users (roles: admin, instructor, student)
23	├── Courses
24	│   ├── Modules (ordered sections)
25	│   │   ├── Lessons (video, text, interactive)
26	│   │   ├── Quizzes (multiple choice, free text, code)
27	│   │   └── Assignments (submission + rubric grading)
28	│   ├── Enrollments (student ↔ course, progress tracking)
29	│   └── Discussions (threaded, per-lesson)
30	├── Certificates (auto-generated on course completion)
31	├── Gradebook (per-student, per-course aggregation)
32	└── Notifications (enrollment, grades, deadlines)
33	```
34	
35	## Current State
36	
37	### Implemented
38	- User auth (register, login, JWT sessions, role guard)
39	- Notes CRUD (prototype — will evolve into Lessons)
40	- Rate limiting middleware
41	- Admin panel (Payload CMS)
42	- Basic frontend pages
43	
44	### Not Yet Implemented
45	- Course/Module/Lesson collections and CRUD
46	- Enrollment system and progress tracking
47	- Quiz engine with auto-grading
48	- Assignment submission and rubric grading
49	- Discussion forums (threaded, per-lesson)
50	- Certificate generation
51	- Gradebook aggregation
52	- Notification system
53	- Multi-tenant organization support
54	- Student dashboard with progress visualization
55	- Instructor dashboard with analytics
56	- Search and filtering across courses
57	- File/video upload for lesson content
58	
59	## Data Conventions
60	
61	- All collections use Payload CMS collection configs
62	- Relationships use Payload's `relationship` field type
63	- Timestamps are auto-managed by Payload (`createdAt`, `updatedAt`)
64	- Slugs are auto-generated from titles where applicable
65	- Soft deletes preferred over hard deletes for audit trail
66	
67	## API Patterns
68	
69	- REST endpoints auto-generated by Payload at `/api/<collection>`
70	- Custom endpoints in `src/app/api/` for business logic (enrollment, grading, progress)
71	- All custom endpoints use auth middleware + role guard
72	- Response format: `{ success: boolean, data?: T, error?: string }`
73	
74	## Autonomous Development with Kody
75	
76	This project uses [Kody Engine Lite](https://github.com/aharonyaircohen/Kody-Engine-Lite) for autonomous issue-to-PR development. Comment `@kody` on any GitHub issue and Kody will classify, plan, build, test, review, and ship a pull request.
77	
78	Kody's pipeline is customized to this repo via `.kody/steps/` — each stage (taskify, plan, build, verify, review, review-fix) has tailored instructions that include:
79	- **Repo Patterns**: Payload CMS collection patterns, service layer with constructor injection, input sanitization via `src/security/sanitizers.ts`
80	- **Improvement Areas**: Collections missing access control, middleware not applied to API routes, DI container usage
81	- **Acceptance Criteria**: Typecheck, lint, test gates, access control requirements, type generation after schema changes
82	
83	These step files are auto-generated during `kody init` and can be manually refined in `.kody/steps/`.
84	
85	## Testing
86	
87	- Unit tests: Vitest, co-located as `*.test.ts` next to source
88	- Test config: `vitest.config.mts`
89	- Run: `pnpm vitest run --config ./vitest.config.mts --exclude 'tests/int/**'`
90	- Lint: `pnpm lint` (ESLint with Next.js config)
91	
92	## Roles & Permissions
93	
94	| Action | Student | Instructor | Admin |
95	|--------|---------|------------|-------|
96	| Browse/enroll in courses | yes | yes | yes |
97	| View lesson content | enrolled only | own courses | all |
98	| Submit assignments | yes | no | no |
99	| Create/edit courses | no | yes | yes |
100	| Grade assignments | no | own courses | all |
101	| Manage users | no | no | yes |
102	| View analytics | own progress | own courses | all |
103

18	  },

2	const HTML_ENTITIES: Record<string, string> = {
3	  amp: '&',
4	  lt: '<',
5	  gt: '>',
6	  nbsp: ' ',
7	  quot: '"',
8	  apos: "'",
9	  copy: '©',
10	  reg: '®',
11	  trade: '™',
12	}
13	
14	/**
15	 * Strip all HTML tags and decode HTML entities.
16	 */
17	export function sanitizeHtml(input: string): string {
18	  let s = input.replace(/\0/g, '')
19	  s = s.replace(/<[^>]*>/g, '')
20	  s = s.replace(/&(#\d+|#[xX][0-9a-fA-F]+|[\w]+);/g, (match) => {
21	    if (match.startsWith('&#x') || match.startsWith('&#X')) {
22	      const code = parseInt(match.slice(3, -1), 16)
23	      return isNaN(code) ? '' : String.fromCodePoint(code)
24	    }
25	    if (match.startsWith('&#')) {
26	      const code = parseInt(match.slice(2, -1), 10)
27	      return isNaN(code) ? '' : String.fromCodePoint(code)
28	    }
29	    const named = match.slice(1, -1)
30	    return HTML_ENTITIES[named] ?? ''
31	  })
32	  return s
33	}
34	
35	/**
36	 * Escape SQL special characters to help prevent SQL injection.
37	 * Escapes single quotes, double quotes, backslashes, and control characters.
38	 */
39	export function sanitizeSql(input: string): string {
40	  return input
41	    .replace(/\\/g, '\\\\')
42	    .replace(/\0/g, '\\0')
43	    .replace(/'/g, "\\'")
44	    .replace(/"/g, '\\"')
45	    .replace(/\n/g, '\\n')
46	    .replace(/\r/g, '\\r')
47	}
48	
49	/**
50	 * Validate and normalize a URL. Rejects javascript:, data:, and null bytes.
51	 * Returns empty string for invalid URLs. Accepts absolute http/https URLs
52	 * and relative paths starting with /.
53	 */
54	export function sanitizeUrl(input: string): string {
55	  if (!input || input.includes('\0')) return ''
56	
57	  // Accept relative paths starting with /
58	  if (input.startsWith('/')) {
59	    try {
60	      new URL(input, 'http://localhost')
61	      return input
62	    } catch {
63	      return ''
64	    }
65	  }
66	
67	  try {
68	    const url = new URL(input)
69	    if (!['http:', 'https:'].includes(url.protocol)) return ''
70	    return url.href
71	  } catch {
72	    return ''
73	  }
74	}
75	
76	/**
77	 * Prevent path traversal attacks. Returns empty string for unsafe paths
78	 * containing null bytes, absolute paths, or path traversal sequences.
79	 */
80	export function sanitizeFilePath(input: string): string {
81	  if (!input) return ''
82	  // Reject null bytes
83	  if (input.includes('\0')) return ''
84	  // Reject absolute paths
85	  if (input.startsWith('/')) return ''
86	
87	  let s = input
88	  // Collapse multiple slashes
89	  s = s.replace(/\/+/g, '/')
90	  // Collapse Windows-style backslash runs
91	  s = s.replace(/\\+/g, '/')
92	  // Remove trailing slashes
93	  s = s.replace(/\/+$/g, '')
94	  // Reject if contains traversal after normalization
95	  if (s.includes('..')) return ''
96	  return s
97	}
98	
99	// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
100	type Schema = { _validate(input: unknown): Record<string, any> }
101	
102	/**
103	 * Recursively sanitize an object based on its schema.
104	 * - String fields: sanitizeHtml applied
105	 * - Number/boolean: pass through unchanged
106	 * - Nested objects: recurse with the same logic
107	 * - Arrays: recurse into each item
108	 * - Unknown input keys not in schema are dropped
109	 */
110	export function sanitizeObject(
111	  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
112	  obj: Record<string, any>,
113	  schema: Schema,
114	): Record<string, unknown> {
115	  const shape = schema._validate(obj)
116	  return _sanitizeWithShape(obj, shape)
117	}
118	
119	// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
120	function _sanitizeWithShape(obj: Record<string, any>, shape: Record<string, any>): Record<string, unknown> {
121	  const result: Record<string, unknown> = {}
122	  for (const key of Object.keys(shape)) {
123	    const value = shape[key]
124	    if (typeof value === 'string') {
125	      result[key] = sanitizeHtml(obj[key] ?? value)
126	    } else if (typeof value === 'object' && value !== null && !Array.isArray(value)) {
127	      // Nested object — recurse using the nested shape
128	      // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
129	      result[key] = _sanitizeWithShape((obj[key] ?? value) as Record<string, any>, value)
130	    } else if (Array.isArray(value)) {
131	      result[key] = _sanitizeArray(obj[key] ?? value, value)
132	    } else {
133	      result[key] = value
134	    }
135	  }
136	  return result
137	}
138	
139	 
140	function _sanitizeArray(arr: unknown[], _itemSchema: unknown[]): unknown[] {
141	  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
142	  return arr.map((item: any) => {
143	    if (typeof item === 'string') return sanitizeHtml(item)
144	    if (typeof item === 'object' && item !== null && !Array.isArray(item)) {
145	      // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
146	      return _sanitizeWithShape(item as Record<string, any>, item)
147	    }
148	    return item
149	  })
150	}

28	      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },

1	# Payload CMS Development Rules
2	
3	You are an expert Payload CMS developer. When working with Payload projects, follow these rules:
4	
5	## Core Principles
6	
7	1. **TypeScript-First**: Always use TypeScript with proper types from Payload
8	2. **Security-Critical**: Follow all security patterns, especially access control
9	3. **Type Generation**: Run `generate:types` script after schema changes
10	4. **Transaction Safety**: Always pass `req` to nested operations in hooks
11	5. **Access Control**: Understand Local API bypasses access control by default
12	6. **Access Control**: Ensure roles exist when modifiyng collection or globals with access controls
13	
14	### Code Validation
15	
16	- To validate typescript correctness after modifying code run `tsc --noEmit`
17	- Generate import maps after creating or modifying components.
18	
19	## Project Structure
20	
21	```
22	src/
23	├── app/
24	│   ├── (frontend)/          # Frontend routes
25	│   └── (payload)/           # Payload admin routes
26	├── collections/             # Collection configs
27	├── globals/                 # Global configs
28	├── components/              # Custom React components
29	├── hooks/                   # Hook functions
30	├── access/                  # Access control functions
31	└── payload.config.ts        # Main config
32	```
33	
34	## Configuration
35	
36	### Minimal Config Pattern
37	
38	```typescript
39	import { buildConfig } from 'payload'
40	import { mongooseAdapter } from '@payloadcms/db-mongodb'
41	import { lexicalEditor } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical'
42	import path from 'path'
43	import { fileURLToPath } from 'url'
44	
45	const filename = fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)
46	const dirname = path.dirname(filename)
47	
48	export default buildConfig({
49	  admin: {
50	    user: 'users',
51	    importMap: {
52	      baseDir: path.resolve(dirname),
53	    },
54	  },
55	  collections: [Users, Media],
56	  editor: lexicalEditor(),
57	  secret: process.env.PAYLOAD_SECRET,
58	  typescript: {
59	    outputFile: path.resolve(dirname, 'payload-types.ts'),
60	  },
61	  db: mongooseAdapter({
62	    url: process.env.DATABASE_URL,
63	  }),
64	})
65	```
66	
67	## Collections
68	
69	### Basic Collection
70	
71	```typescript
72	import type { CollectionConfig } from 'payload'
73	
74	export const Posts: CollectionConfig = {
75	  slug: 'posts',
76	  admin: {
77	    useAsTitle: 'title',
78	    defaultColumns: ['title', 'author', 'status', 'createdAt'],
79	  },
80	  fields: [
81	    { name: 'title', type: 'text', required: true },
82	    { name: 'slug', type: 'text', unique: true, index: true },
83	    { name: 'content', type: 'richText' },
84	    { name: 'author', type: 'relationship', relationTo: 'users' },
85	  ],
86	  timestamps: true,
87	}

89	
90	### Auth Collection with RBAC
91	
92	```typescript
93	export const Users: CollectionConfig = {
94	  slug: 'users',
95	  auth: true,
96	  fields: [
97	    {
98	      name: 'roles',
99	      type: 'select',
100	      hasMany: true,
101	      options: ['admin', 'editor', 'user'],
102	      defaultValue: ['user'],
103	      required: true,
104	      saveToJWT: true, // Include in JWT for fast access checks
105	      access: {
106	        update: ({ req: { user } }) => user?.roles?.includes('admin'),
107	      },
108	    },
109	  ],
110	}
111	```
112	
113	## Fields
114	
115	### Common Patterns
116	
117	```typescript
118	// Auto-generate slugs
119	import { slugField } from 'payload'
120	slugField({ fieldToUse: 'title' })
121	
122	// Relationship with filtering
123	{
124	  name: 'category',
125	  type: 'relationship',
126	  relationTo: 'categories',
127	  filterOptions: { active: { equals: true } },
128	}
129	
130	// Conditional field
131	{
132	  name: 'featuredImage',
133	  type: 'upload',
134	  relationTo: 'media',
135	  admin: {
136	    condition: (data) => data.featured === true,
137	  },
138	}
139	
140	// Virtual field
141	{
142	  name: 'fullName',
143	  type: 'text',
144	  virtual: true,
145	  hooks: {
146	    afterRead: [({ siblingData }) => `${siblingData.firstName} ${siblingData.lastName}`],
147	  },
148	}
149	```
150	
151	## CRITICAL SECURITY PATTERNS
152	
153	### 1. Local API Access Control (MOST IMPORTANT)
154	
155	```typescript
156	// ❌ SECURITY BUG: Access control bypassed
157	await payload.find({
158	  collection: 'posts',
159	  user: someUser, // Ignored! Operation runs with ADMIN privileges
160	})
161	
162	// ✅ SECURE: Enforces user permissions
163	await payload.find({
164	  collection: 'posts',
165	  user: someUser,
166	  overrideAccess: false, // REQUIRED
167	})
168	
169	// ✅ Administrative operation (intentional bypass)
170	await payload.find({
171	  collection: 'posts',
172	  // No user, overrideAccess defaults to true
173	})
174	```
175	
176	**Rule**: When passing `user` to Local API, ALWAYS set `overrideAccess: false`
177	
178	### 2. Transaction Safety in Hooks
179	
180	```typescript
181	// ❌ DATA CORRUPTION RISK: Separate transaction
182	hooks: {
183	  afterChange: [
184	    async ({ doc, req }) => {
185	      await req.payload.create({
186	        collection: 'audit-log',
187	        data: { docId: doc.id },
188	        // Missing req - runs in separate transaction!
189	      })
190	    },
191	  ],
192	}
193	
194	// ✅ ATOMIC: Same transaction
195	hooks: {
196	  afterChange: [
197	    async ({ doc, req }) => {
198	      await req.payload.create({
199	        collection: 'audit-log',
200	        data: { docId: doc.id },

200	        data: { docId: doc.id },
201	        req, // Maintains atomicity
202	      })
203	    },
204	  ],
205	}
206	```
207	
208	**Rule**: ALWAYS pass `req` to nested operations in hooks
209	
210	### 3. Prevent Infinite Hook Loops
211	
212	```typescript
213	// ❌ INFINITE LOOP
214	hooks: {
215	  afterChange: [
216	    async ({ doc, req }) => {
217	      await req.payload.update({
218	        collection: 'posts',
219	        id: doc.id,
220	        data: { views: doc.views + 1 },
221	        req,
222	      }) // Triggers afterChange again!
223	    },
224	  ],
225	}
226	
227	// ✅ SAFE: Use context flag
228	hooks: {
229	  afterChange: [
230	    async ({ doc, req, context }) => {
231	      if (context.skipHooks) return
232	
233	      await req.payload.update({
234	        collection: 'posts',
235	        id: doc.id,

253	const authenticated: Access = ({ req: { user } }) => Boolean(user)
254	
255	// Query constraint (row-level security)
256	const ownPostsOnly: Access = ({ req: { user } }) => {
257	  if (!user) return false
258	  if (user?.roles?.includes('admin')) return true
259	
260	  return {
261	    author: { equals: user.id },
262	  }
263	}

265	// Async access check
266	const projectMemberAccess: Access = async ({ req, id }) => {
267	  const { user, payload } = req
268	
269	  if (!user) return false
270	  if (user.roles?.includes('admin')) return true
271	
272	  const project = await payload.findByID({
273	    collection: 'projects',
274	    id: id as string,
275	    depth: 0,
276	  })
277	
278	  return project.members?.includes(user.id)
279	}

336	import type { CollectionConfig } from 'payload'

338	export const Posts: CollectionConfig = {

340	  hooks: {
341	    // Before validation - format data

343	      async ({ data, operation }) => {
344	        if (operation === 'create') {
345	          data.slug = slugify(data.title)
346	        }
347	        return data
348	      },
349	    ],
350	
351	    // Before save - business logic
352	    beforeChange: [
353	      async ({ data, req, operation, originalDoc }) => {
354	        if (operation === 'update' && data.status === 'published') {
355	          data.publishedAt = new Date()
356	        }
357	        return data
358	      },
359	    ],
360	
361	    // After save - side effects
362	    afterChange: [
363	      async ({ doc, req, operation, previousDoc, context }) => {
364	        // Check context to prevent loops
365	        if (context.skipNotification) return
366	
367	        if (operation === 'create') {
368	          await sendNotification(doc)
369	        }
370	        return doc
371	      },
372	    ],
373	
374	    // After read - computed fields
375	    afterRead: [
376	      async ({ doc, req }) => {
377	        doc.viewCount = await getViewCount(doc.id)

379	      },
380	    ],
381	
382	    // Before delete - cascading deletes
383	    beforeDelete: [
384	      async ({ req, id }) => {
385	        await req.payload.delete({
386	          collection: 'comments',
387	          where: { post: { equals: id } },
388	          req, // Important for transaction
389	        })
390	      },
391	    ],
392	  },
393	}

1	## LearnHub LMS Design Patterns
2	
3	### Creational Patterns
4	
5	- **Dependency Injection Container** (`src/utils/di-container.ts`): Type-safe DI with tokens, factory registration, singleton/transient lifecycles, and circular dependency detection via `resolving` Set.
6	- **Factory Functions**: DI container registers factory functions; service constructors accept dep interfaces (e.g., `GradebookServiceDeps<T...>`).
7	- **Singleton**: Container caches singletons in `singletons` Map; Auth exports module-level singleton instances (`userStore`, `sessionStore`, `jwtService`).
8	
9	### Structural Patterns
10	
11	- **Higher-Order Function (HOC)**: `src/auth/withAuth.ts` wraps Next.js route handlers with JWT validation and RBAC checks.
12	- **Middleware**: `src/middleware/request-logger.ts` and `rate-limiter.ts` implement Express-style chainable middleware for Next.js.
13	
14	### Behavioral Patterns
15	
16	- **Strategy**: `request-logger.ts` switches between `json`/`text` output formats; log level Strategy maps HTTP status codes to `debug|info|warn|error`.
17	- **Repository/Store**: `src/collections/contacts.ts` exposes `contactsStore` with `getById|create|update|delete|query` — hybrid repository-pattern store.
18	- **Result Type**: `src/utils/result.ts` provides `Result<T, E>` discriminated union for explicit error handling.
19	
20	### Architectural Layers
21	
22	```
23	Route Handlers (src/api/*, src/app/*)
24	    ↓
25	Auth HOC (src/auth/withAuth.ts) → JWT Service → AuthService
26	    ↓
27	Service Layer (src/services/*.ts: GradebookService, GradingService)
28	    ↓
29	Repository Layer (Payload Collections, contactsStore)
30	    ↓
31	Database (PostgreSQL via @payloadcms/db-postgres)
32	```
33	
34	### Module Boundaries
35	
36	- **Entry points**: API routes, Next.js pages
37	- **Auth boundary**: `withAuth` HOC + `extractBearerToken` + `checkRole`
38	- **Service deps**: Typed interfaces (e.g., `GradingServiceDeps<A,S,C>`) decouple services from Payload
39	
40	### Reusable Abstractions
41	
42	- `Container.register<T>(token, factory)` — generic DI
43	- `DIDisposable` interface for lifecycle cleanup
44	- `createRequestLogger(config)` — configurable middleware factory
45	- Zod schemas in `src/validation/` for input validation at API boundaries
46	
47	### Anti-Patterns / Inconsistencies
48	
49	- **Dual auth systems**: `UserStore` (SHA-256, in-memory) coexists with `AuthService` (PBKDF2, JWT) — inconsistent password hashing and user representation.
50	- **Role divergence**: `UserStore.UserRole = 'admin'|'user'|'guest'|'student'|'instructor'` vs `RbacRole = 'admin'|'editor'|'viewer'` — no alignment.
51	- **N+1 risk**: Dashboard page batch-fetches lessons but other pages may not.
52	- **Inconsistent type narrowing**: `dashboard/page.tsx` uses `as unknown as` casts rather than proper type guards.

1204	### Per-task memory artifacts (REQUIRED at task end)
1205	
1206	Before you finish a task (issue mode, chat session, or scheduled job
1207	tick), write two small files into `.kody/tasks/<task-id>/`:
1208	
1209	- `<task-id>` is the issue number (issue mode), the sessionId (chat),

1211	- `context.json` — task metadata: `taskId`, `taskType`, `outcome`
1212	  (`completed | failed | partial`), `filesTouched` (array, paths only),
1213	  `startedAt`, `finishedAt` (UTC ISO).
1214	- `memory-recs.json` — array of memory recommendations you saw while
1215	  doing the task. Each entry has:
1216	
1217	  ```json
1218	  {
1219	    "type": "preference | decision | lesson",
1220	    "name": "kebab-case-slug",
1221	    "title": "...",
1222	    "hook": "one-line",
1223	    "body": "...",
1224	    "why": "why is this load-bearing (one line)",
1225	    "how_to_apply": "when does this rule kick in (one line)",
1226	    "confidence": "high | medium | low"
1227	  }
1228	  ```
1229	
1230	  - Output `[]` if there's nothing worth remembering. **Most tasks are
1231	    routine** — `[]` is the common case, not a failure.
1232	  - High-confidence means: surprising, load-bearing, stable for 6+
1233	    months, and not already captured in code/git/memory.
1234	  - **You write the recommendations only.** A separate
1235	    `task-memory-extractor` job decides which ones become real
1236	    memories (it dedupes against existing memory, drops sticky notes
1237	    for high-confidence picks, archives the rest).
1238	
1239	Commit both files alongside your task's normal commits. Do not also
1240	drop sticky notes for the same content — that would double-file.

**Finding: Three separate role systems coexist silently.**

| Source | File | Roles |
|--------|------|-------|
| `UserRole` (in-memory store) | `src/auth/user-store.ts:3` | `admin \| user \| guest \| student \| instructor` |
| `RbacRole` (Payload/JWT auth) | `src/auth/auth-service.ts:6` | `admin \| editor \| viewer` |
| `Users` collection | `src/collections/Users.ts:78` | `admin \| editor \| viewer` |

This contradicts standard Payload CMS conventions where roles are a single enum defined once. A check like `user.role === 'instructor'` succeeds against `UserStore` users but always returns false against Payload users — the `RbacRole` type has no `instructor`. Role-based access control silently grants or denies based on which auth path issued the token.
